behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module boundary (git-derived branch facts + PR-state provider + staleness rule), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real HTTP/subprocess; in-process wiring can reproduce the core failure. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit mocks can hide bad field handoff (e.g., age or ahead-count dropped), so Unit is too low. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATES: Low(Unit) setup: call rule function with handcrafted state; assertion: stale=true; catches boolean logic bug; misses integration bug where collector passes wrong age units. Current(Integration) setup: wire collectors/services with fake git graph + fake PR store; assertion: only branches meeting all 3 predicates flagged; catches state-handoff and predicate-composition bugs; misses git CLI parsing/auth failures. High(System) setup: temp repo + real git commands + real PR API; assertion: same; catches infra/parsing issues; misses little beyond current for stated behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require "live repo" or real network/subprocess execution.
behaviors.test_description: Create an integration test that wires branch-facts computation and PR-state lookup into the staleness detector using deterministic fakes for git/PR data. Include cases for (ahead+old+no-PR), (ahead+young+no-PR), (ahead+old+open-PR), and (not-ahead+old+no-PR), and assert only the first is labeled stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is wiring between startup hook, staleness service, and message renderer/context builder. Unit would miss miswired startup conditions. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: mocking only final formatter can hide missing context propagation (branch name/age/PR hint). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATES: Low(Unit) setup: call formatter with stale state; assertion: string contains warning; catches template bug; misses startup hook not invoking detector. Current(Integration) setup: run session-start initialization with injected stale state provider; assertion: one warning emitted with branch context fields; catches hook-registration and context-handoff bugs; misses terminal/transport edge cases. High(System) setup: launch full CLI session process; assertion: startup output contains warning; catches process/bootstrap issues; misses little extra for stated behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not require real subprocess/OS semantics, only session-start orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run session bootstrap in-process with a stub detector returning stale for current branch, capture emitted notifications, and assert warning presence plus actionable context (branch name, age/threshold, and next-step hint). Also assert no warning when detector returns clean.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly a local classifier function; no cross-module boundary required. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: not applicable; no dependency to hide failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATES: Low(Unit) setup: table-driven inputs to classifier; assertion: exact label per case; catches condition-order and enum-mapping bugs; misses upstream state computation bugs. Current(Unit) same as low (already minimum). High(Integration) setup: include upstream state builder; assertion classifier result; catches contract mismatch; misses nothing specific in classifier logic beyond Unit. REJECTION-GATE: rejected higher levels because behavior text says "local status-classifier function" and deterministic label mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Add table-driven unit tests over boundary states (no commits ahead, ahead but young, ahead+old+no-PR, ahead+old+open-PR) and assert exact label outputs, including deterministic repeat runs.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets command output from end-to-end wiring plus live state sources. REAL-INFRA: explicit "against live repo and PR state" requires real git/repo and real PR API data; System floor. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo/PR responses can pass while real auth, pagination, branch-resolution, or rendering integration fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model dependency. CANDIDATES: Low(Integration) setup: in-process command handler with stub repo/PR adapters; assertion rendered status text; catches formatting/branching bug; misses live API/repo resolution failures. Current(System) setup: run command in real repo with controlled branch conditions and real PR state; assertion rendered line is clean vs stale; catches real adapter/auth/query/path bugs. High(Agentic) setup: involve LLM interpretation of output; catches none relevant; misses nothing additional for core behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due concrete phrase "against live repo and PR state" which requires real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repository environment with real PR lookup enabled (or real staging endpoint), create one clean branch and one ahead+no-PR stale branch, and assert rendered output labels each correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is contract mapping between PR-state resolution and stale classifier across modules. Unit is too low if only classifier is tested with idealized flags. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require live network in wording; deterministic PR-state fixtures can expose false-positive logic. MOCK-HIDE: over-mocking can hide status mapping mismatch (e.g., merged interpreted as none), so integration wiring is needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATES: Low(Unit) setup: classifier input includes prStatus=open/merged; assertion non-stale; catches local predicate bug; misses bug where PR resolver emits different status shape. Current(Integration) setup: wire PR resolver adapter contract + staleness detector with fixture PR records (open, merged, none); assertion only none can become stale; catches enum/contract mismatch bugs; misses live API pagination/auth issues. High(System) setup: real GitHub API with actual open/merged PRs; assertion same; catches transport/query issues not required by behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text focuses on logical false positives, not live API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test staleness evaluation with fixture branches paired to PR states `open`, `merged`, and `none`; assert `open`/`merged` never produce `stale-no-pr` even when branch is old and ahead, while `none` can.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure mode depends on real VCS history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry semantics, which are hard to faithfully fake. REAL-INFRA: requires real git operations/graph evolution; subprocess/OS-level git behavior places this at System. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit graphs often miss edge cases (rewritten SHAs, equivalent content merged) that cause stale false positives. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATES: Low(Integration) setup: synthetic commit graph objects representing pre/post rebase; assertion non-stale after merge; catches simple ancestry logic bug; misses real git rewrite edge semantics. Current(System) setup: create repo, branch, commit, rebase or squash-merge into main, then run detector; assertion branch not stale-no-pr; catches real ancestry/rewrite misclassification bugs. High(Workflow) setup: multi-stage agent pipeline around detection; catches nothing additional for this behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly names rebase/squash hash changes, which are concrete real-git transformation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git test repo, script scenarios for (a) rebase feature onto updated main and merge, (b) squash merge feature into main, then evaluate staleness for original branch refs and assert detector does not classify them as unPRed stale work after merge-equivalent state.
